tasks.task: Create MailModule with Dynamic Provider Injection

acceptanceCriteria.ac: Email Provider Interface oluşturulmuş (`src/modules/mail/interfaces/mail-provider.interface.ts`) - Interface: `IEmailProvider { send(to, subject, html, text?): Promise }` - EmailResult interface: `{ messageId: string, success: boolean }`

SendGrid Provider oluşturulmuş (`src/modules/mail/providers/sendgrid.provider.ts`) - SendGrid SDK configured (@sendgrid/mail) - Environment variables: SENDGRID_API_KEY, MAIL_FROM - Implements IEmailProvider interface - `send(to, subject, html, text?): Promise` method - Error handling: Provider exceptions wrapped
MailModule dynamic provider injection - MAIL_PROVIDER env var ile provider seçimi (default: 'sendgrid') - Dynamic provider injection pattern (factory pattern) - MailModule'de provider configuration

MailService oluşturulmuş (provider-agnostic) - `src/modules/mail/services/mail.service.ts` - `sendEmail(to: string, subject: string, html: string, text?: string): Promise` method - Provider-agnostic implementation (IEmailProvider interface kullanır) - Error handling: Provider exceptions wrapped ve logged

doc.title: Epic 5: Communication Infrastructure
doc.section: Story 5.2: Email Provider Interface & SendGrid Implementation
doc.snippet: Email provider abstraction via IEmailProvider interface. SendGrid SDK configured with SENDGRID_API_KEY and MAIL_FROM. MailModule dynamic provider injection via MAIL_PROVIDER env var. MailService provider-agnostic implementation.

doc.title: Epic 5 Technical Specification
doc.section: Story 5.2: Email Provider Interface & SendGrid Implementation
doc.snippet: Complete AC specifications (AC-5.2.1 through AC-5.2.4). MailService and SendGridProvider design. Provider abstraction pattern. IEmailProvider interface specification. SendGrid API integration details.

doc.title: Story 5.1: SMS Provider Interface
doc.snippet: Provider abstraction pattern established: Interface-based provider abstraction, environment variable-based provider selection, dynamic provider injection via factory pattern. Module structure pattern: Standard NestJS module structure with clear separation (Interface → Provider → Service).

doc.title: Implementation Patterns
doc.snippet: Provider exceptions wrapped in service layer. Network failures: Log error, don't block main flow. Invalid credentials: Throw BadRequestException with i18n translated message.

doc.title: Testing Strategy
doc.snippet: Pattern: Arrange-Act-Assert. Mock dependencies (ConfigService, external SDKs). Minimum coverage: 70%. Services: 80%+ coverage. Controllers: 70%+ coverage.

artifact.reason: Reference for module structure pattern: providers array, exports array, imports array. Similar structure needed for MailModule.
Reference for service pattern: Injectable decorator, Logger usage, dependency injection (PrismaService, FonivaService), error handling with i18n. Similar pattern for MailService.

Reference for provider pattern: Injectable decorator, ConfigService injection for environment variables, constructor initialization with config validation, error handling. Similar pattern for SendGridProvider.
Reference for ConfigModule setup: ConfigModule.forRoot with isGlobal: true, load array for config factories. MailModule will need to import ConfigModule similarly.
Reference for environment variable validation: Joi schema pattern. Need to add SENDGRID_API_KEY, MAIL_FROM, MAIL_PROVIDER validation.

constraints.constraint: Provider Abstraction Pattern: Email provider abstraction via IEmailProvider interface (similar to SMS provider pattern from Story 5.1). Dynamic provider injection via environment variable (MAIL_PROVIDER). Easy provider switching: SendGrid → AWS SES (future story). [Source: docs/tech-spec-epic-5.md#System-Architecture-Alignment]

Module Structure Pattern: Follow standard NestJS module structure: `src/modules/mail/` with interfaces/, providers/, services/. Similar to SMS module structure from Story 5.1. Clear separation: Interface → Provider → Service. [Source: docs/epics/epic-5-communication-infrastructure-foniva-sms-pattern.md#Story-5.2]

Error Handling Pattern: Provider exceptions wrapped in MailService. Log errors to Winston logger (Epic 7). Throw BadRequestException with i18n translated message. Don't expose provider-specific error details to clients. [Source: docs/architecture/implementation-patterns-ai-agent-consistency-rules.md#Error-Handling-Pattern]

Environment Configuration Pattern: Environment variables: SENDGRID_API_KEY, MAIL_FROM, MAIL_PROVIDER. ConfigService injection for type-safe config access. Validation on module initialization. [Source: docs/tech-spec-epic-5.md#External-Service-Integrations]

Service Layer Pattern: MailService is provider-agnostic (depends on IEmailProvider interface, not concrete provider). Provider implementation details hidden from consumers. Easy to test (mock IEmailProvider interface). [Source: docs/tech-spec-epic-5.md#Services-and-Modules]

Testing Constraint: Unit tests: Mock dependencies (ConfigService, external SDKs). Integration tests: Test real API calls with test credentials. E2E tests: Complete user flows. Minimum coverage: 70%. [Source: docs/architecture/testing-strategy.md]

Dependency Constraint: ConfigService (Epic 1) required for environment variable access. Winston Logger (Epic 7) required for error logging. i18n (Epic 7) required for internationalized error messages. [Source: docs/stories/5-2-email-provider-interface-sendgrid-implementation.md#Dependencies-from-Previous-Stories]

No Database Dependency: Email sending is stateless, no email tracking entity in this story. MailService does not require PrismaService. [Source: docs/stories/5-2-email-provider-interface-sendgrid-implementation.md#Module-Dependencies]

interface.name: IEmailProvider
ConfigService.get()
interface.signature: get<T = string>(key: string, defaultValue?: T): T | undefined // Examples: configService.get<string>('SENDGRID_API_KEY') configService.get<string>('MAIL_FROM') configService.get<string>('MAIL_PROVIDER', 'sendgrid')

interface.name: SendGrid SDK
MailService.sendEmail()
interface.signature: sendEmail(to: string, subject: string, html: string, text?: string): Promise<void>

interface.name: MailModule Provider Factory

tests.standards: Testing follows Arrange-Act-Assert pattern. Unit tests mock dependencies (ConfigService, external SDKs). Integration tests use real API calls with test credentials. E2E tests cover complete user flows. Minimum coverage: 70%. Services require 80%+ coverage. Controllers require 70%+ coverage. [Source: docs/architecture/testing-strategy.md]

ideas.test: Unit test: IEmailProvider interface contract validation. Verify interface structure matches specification.

Unit test SendGridProvider.send(): Mock SendGrid SDK, test API call with correct parameters (to, from, subject, html, text), test error handling (invalid API key, network failures), verify EmailResult return format.
Unit test MailModule provider injection: Test factory function returns SendGridProvider when MAIL_PROVIDER=sendgrid, test error when unsupported provider specified.
Unit test MailService.sendEmail(): Mock IEmailProvider, test delegation to provider.send(), test error wrapping and logging, test BadRequestException on provider failure.
Integration test: MailService with SendGridProvider using real SendGrid API call (test environment), test invalid SENDGRID_API_KEY throws BadRequestException, test network failure handled gracefully.
E2E test: Email sending flow - MailService.sendEmail() → SendGrid API → Email delivered. Error flow - Invalid API key → Error logged → BadRequestException thrown.
